title: Gymnadenia runei
intro: Gymnadenia runei, commonly known as the green orchid, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant in the genus Gymnadenia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Gymnadenia runei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenylethanoid glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnadenia runei is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, although its use is mostly limited to research and experimental purposes due to its rarity and limited availability.
taxonomy: Gymnadenia runei is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, specifically within the genus Gymnadenia. It is a type of terrestrial orchid native to Europe. Gymnadenia runei is often found growing in grasslands and heathlands, typically at high elevations. The plant is known to bloom in mid to late summer, producing white or pinkish flowers. Gymnadenia runei is considered a rare and endangered species in some parts of its range due to habitat loss and degradation.
